content:
**January 20, 2025 — Washington, D.C.**

**Kamala Harris Sworn In as 47th President of the United States**

In a historic ceremony on the steps of the U.S. Capitol, Kamala Harris was inaugurated today as the 47th President of the United States, following her victory in the November 2024 election. Harris, the first woman and first person of South Asian and African American descent to hold the office, took the oath administered by Chief Justice John Roberts shortly after noon.

President Harris addressed a crowd of thousands, calling for “unity, resilience, and a renewed commitment to American democracy.” In her inaugural speech, she said, “Today, we turn the page and move forward together, building a nation where every voice is heard and every opportunity is open to all.”

The ceremony was attended by her predecessor, President Joe Biden, who did not seek re-election, and other former presidents, as well as dignitaries from around the world. Outgoing Republican nominee Ron DeSantis congratulated Harris, saying in a statement, “Though we differed on policy, I respect the will of the American people and wish her success in guiding our country.”

Markets responded with cautious optimism as Harris reaffirmed commitments to economic stability and bipartisan cooperation. As the new administration begins, Americans and the world watch closely to see how President Harris charts the course for the next four years.
